missions.name: She Has Been Seen at the Co-orbitals
missions.briefing: Young pilot. A standard gather cycle — heavy regolith, Saturn co-orbitals, routine volume. The rates are good if you move clean and I am told you generally do. Land, fill the hold, come back. I shall not take up more of your time than the work requires. — Finch

missions.name: A Note at the Trojans
missions.briefing: Young pilot. A three-stage gravimetric pass on a candidate body in the Saturn co-orbitals — probe calibration, standard hold times. The body is well-behaved; the surface is clean and the approach is clean. If the data comes back without incident I have more of the same at the same rate. Standard pay plus travel. I do not expect surprises. — Finch

missions.name: A Compositional Note
missions.briefing: Young pilot. A buyer wants a compositional note before committing to the body. Nothing theatrical: read several rocks, return the report, and recover the sample the report indicates. The distance premium is already included. — Finch

missions.name: The Trail Goes Cold Near Saturn
missions.briefing: Young pilot. A buyer wants certified photometric data before committing to extraction. Standoff approach, probe deploy, hold the lander stable through the exposure. Nothing unusual about the body — it simply needs a clean telemetry packet before anyone signs anything. Pays above standard because of the distance. Return with good data. — Finch
